links.title: mistralai/Mistral-Medium-3.5-128B

primary_link.title: mistralai/Mistral-Medium-3.5-128B

mistral-ai.summary: Mistral Medium 3.5 is our first flagship merged model. It is a dense 128B model with a 256k context window, handling instruction-following, reasoning, and coding in a single set of weights. Mistral Medium 3.5 replaces its predecessor Mistral Medium 3.1 and Magistral in Le Chat. It also replaces Devstral 2 in our coding agent Vibe. Concretely, expect better performance for instruct, reasoning and coding tasks in a new unified model in comparison with our previous released models. Reasoning effort is configurable per request, so the same model can answer a quick chat reply or work through a complex agentic run. We trained the vision encoder from scratch to handle variable image sizes and aspect ratios. Find more information on our blog. > To speed up local inference using vLLM or SGLang,...

Power sampling provides a principled way to bias decoding toward such modes by targeting p_theta(x)^alpha with alpha > 1, but practical approximations must account for future-dependent correction factors that determine which prefixes remain promising.

links.title: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment

primary_link.title: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment

top5.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models aim to provide a single generalist controller for robots, but today's systems fall short on the criteria that matter for real-world deployment. Frontier models are closed, open-weight alternatives are tied to expensive hardware, reasoning-augmented policies pay prohibitive latency for their grounding, and fine-tuned success rates remain below the threshold for dependable use. We present MolmoAct2, a fully open action reasoning model built for practical deploym
top5.summary_zh: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) 模型旨在為機器人提供單一的通用控制器，但目前的系統在實際部署的關鍵標準上表現不足。領先模型是閉源的，開源權重替代方案則受限於昂貴的硬體，基於推理的策略為了其 grounding 付出過高的延遲，而 fine-tuned 的成功率仍低於可靠使用的門檻。我們提出了 MolmoAct2，這是一個為實際部署而建構的完全開源的動作推理模型。
top5.title: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment
top5.title_zh: MolmoAct2：用於實際部署的動作推理模型

links.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents

primary_link.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents

top5.summary: Benchmarks within the OpenClaw ecosystem have thus far evaluated exclusively assistant-level tasks, leaving the academic-level capabilities of OpenClaw largely unexamined. We introduce AcademiClaw, a bilingual benchmark of 80 complex, long-horizon tasks sourced directly from university students' real academic workflows -- homework, research projects, competitions, and personal projects -- that they found current AI agents unable to solve effectively. Curated from 230 student-submitted candidates
top5.summary_zh: OpenClaw 生態系統內的基準測試迄今為止僅評估了助理級任務，OpenClaw 的學術級能力在很大程度上仍未被檢視。我們推出了 AcademiClaw，這是一個包含 80 個複雜、長程任務的雙語基準，這些任務直接來源於大學生實際的學術工作流程——包括作業、研究項目、競賽和個人專案——這些學生發現目前的 AI Agents 無法有效解決這些任務。該基準從 230 個學生提交的候選任務中篩選而成。
top5.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
top5.title_zh: AcademiClaw：當學生為 AI Agents 設定挑戰

primary_link.title: Autonomous LLM Agent Worms: Cross-Platform Propagation, Automated Discovery and Temporal Re-Entry Defense

top5.summary: Autonomous LLM agents operate as long-running processes with persistent workspaces, memory files, scheduled task state, and messaging integrations. These features create a new propagation risk: attacker-influenced content can be written into persistent agent state, re-enter the LLM decision context through scheduled autoloading, and drive high-risk actions including configuration changes and cross-agent transmission. top5.summary_zh: 自主 LLM Agents 作為長時間執行的程序運作，具有持久性工作區、記憶體文件、排程任務狀態和訊息整合。這些功能產生了一種新的傳播風險：受攻擊者影響的內容可以寫入持久性的 Agent 狀態，透過排程的自動加載重新進入 LLM 決策情境，並驅動高風險行為，包括配置更改和跨 Agent 傳輸。我們提出了第一個用於自動分析持久性

links.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits

primary_link.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits

top5.summary: Language models (LMs) are increasingly used in high-stakes, multi-agent settings, where following instructions and maintaining value alignment are critical. Most alignment research focuses on interactions between a single LM and a single user, failing to address the risk of misaligned behavior spreading between multiple LMs in multi-turn interactions. We find evidence of this phenomenon, which we call misalignment contagion, across multiple LMs as they engage multi-turn conversational social dil
top5.summary_zh: Language models (LMs) 越來越多地用於高風險、multi-agent 環境中，在這些環境中，遵循指令和維持價值 alignment 至關重要。大多數 alignment 研究都集中在單個 LM 和單個用戶之間的互動，未能解決在多輪互動中多個 LMs 之間 misaligned 行為傳播的風險。我們發現了這種現象的證據，我們稱之為 misalignment contagion，它發生在多個 LMs 進行 multi-turn 對話式社會困境時。
top5.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits
top5.title_zh: 透過隱式特徵引導來緩解 misalignment 傳染
